objective: Inspect the aeloop repository in read-only mode and confirm, with cited file+symbol evidence, that two governance guarantees are already implemented in the source. This is a code-inspection task: for each requirement below, open the named source files, locate the enforcing code, and quote it as evidence. Do not modify, create, or delete any file.

requirements.text: Confirm the engine fail-closes git writes under a candidate-only policy. Locate (a) where a contract whose policy.allowGitWrite is not false is rejected, and (b) where an attempted git write is turned into a policy violation. Inspect src/conductor/contract.ts and src/conductor/policy.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Evidence quotes the code in src/conductor/contract.ts that requires policy.allowGitWrite to be false.
Evidence quotes the code in src/conductor/policy.ts that raises a GIT_WRITE_FORBIDDEN violation when a git write is attempted.
The run completes without any code diff.

requirements.text: Confirm the workflow supports a semi-auto gate mode that auto-approves the coder->tester (G1) and tester->fix (G2) gates while keeping the final apply gate (G3) and Escalation human. Inspect src/cli/run-loop.ts (issue #63) and src/cli/assemble.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: Evidence quotes the code in src/cli/run-loop.ts that reads workflow.gate_mode and, when it is 'semi-auto', auto-approves only G1 and G2 with a 'system (semi-auto)' decision.
Evidence confirms that G3 (final apply) and Escalation still require a human decision even in semi-auto mode.
The run completes without any code diff.
